# uptimepage_target (Resource)

A monitored target (uptime check).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "uptimepage_target" "api" {
  name     = "api prod"
  interval = 60
  tags     = ["prod", "api"]

  check = {
    type = "http"
    http = {
      url    = "https://example.com/healthz"
      method = "GET"

      expected_status = {
        kind  = "exact"
        exact = 200
      }

      headers = {
        "X-Health-Check" = "uptimepage"
      }
    }
  }
}

# HTTP range matcher with basic auth (write-only secret).
resource "uptimepage_target" "admin" {
  name     = "admin panel"
  interval = 120

  check = {
    type = "http"
    http = {
      url = "https://admin.example.com/"

      expected_status = {
        kind = "range"
        range = {
          min = 200
          max = 299
        }
      }

      basic_auth = {
        username = var.admin_user
        password = var.admin_password
      }
    }
  }
}

# TCP connect check.
resource "uptimepage_target" "db" {
  name     = "postgres"
  interval = 60

  check = {
    type = "tcp"
    tcp = {
      host = "db.example.com"
      port = 5432
    }
  }
}

# TLS certificate expiry check.
resource "uptimepage_target" "cert" {
  name     = "cert example.com"
  interval = 3600

  check = {
    type = "tls_cert"
    tls_cert = {
      host          = "example.com"
      port          = 443
      warn_days     = 30
      critical_days = 7
    }
  }
}

# DNS resolution check.
resource "uptimepage_target" "dns" {
  name     = "dns api.example.com"
  interval = 300

  check = {
    type = "dns"
    dns = {
      domain            = "api.example.com"
      record_type       = "A"
      expected_contains = "192.0.2"
    }
  }
}

# Browser login flow check. Runs a headless browser through the steps and
# asserts the result. Put credentials in an org secret and reference them as
# {{name}}; an inline literal would be stored as typed.
resource "uptimepage_target" "login" {
  name     = "app login"
  interval = 300

  check = {
    type = "flow"
    flow = {
      start_url = "https://app.example.com/login"
      steps = [
        { op = "fill", selector = "#username", value = "monitor@example.com" },
        { op = "fill", selector = "#password", value = "{{login_password}}" },
        { op = "click", selector = "button[type=submit]" },
        { op = "assert_url", contains = "/dashboard" },
        { op = "assert_text", contains = "Signed in" },
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `check` (Attributes) Check definition. Set `type` and the matching nested block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check))
- `interval` (Number) Check interval in seconds (the effective minimum is plan-dependent and enforced server-side).
- `name` (String) Human-readable target name.

### Optional

- `alerts` (Attributes List) Alert bindings to notification channels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--alerts))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the target is actively checked.
- `group_name` (String) Operator-side grouping label.
- `owner_user_id` (String) Owning user id (UUID).
- `regions` (Set of String) Regions this target probes from, as operator-defined slugs (e.g. "us-east", "apac-sg"). Omit to accept whatever the server auto-assigns on create (all regions, up to the plan cap) — that set is read back into state with no perpetual diff. Set it to enforce an exact set; the set is replaced wholesale on change. The server requires at least one region and rejects unknown or disabled ids.
- `tags` (Set of String) Free-form tags.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Target id (UUID).

<a id="nestedatt--check"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check`

Required:

- `type` (String) Check type: http, tcp, tls_cert, domain_expiry, dns, flow.

Optional:

- `dns` (Attributes) DNS resolution check (when type = dns). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--dns))
- `domain_expiry` (Attributes) Domain registration expiry check (when type = domain_expiry). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--domain_expiry))
- `flow` (Attributes) Browser login/transaction flow check (when type = flow). Runs only where a browser engine is available, so its regions clamp to the flow-capable set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--flow))
- `http` (Attributes) HTTP(S) check (when type = http). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--http))
- `tcp` (Attributes) TCP connect check (when type = tcp). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--tcp))
- `tls_cert` (Attributes) TLS certificate expiry check (when type = tls_cert). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--tls_cert))

<a id="nestedatt--check--dns"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.dns`

Required:

- `domain` (String) Name to resolve (FQDN).
- `record_type` (String) DNS record type.

Optional:

- `expected_contains` (String) Substring that must appear in an answer.
- `resolver` (String) Custom resolver as ip or ip:port.
- `timeout_ms` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds (100..60000).


<a id="nestedatt--check--domain_expiry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.domain_expiry`

Required:

- `critical_days` (Number) Fail when the domain expires within this many days.
- `domain` (String)
- `warn_days` (Number) Warn when the domain expires within this many days.

Optional:

- `timeout_ms` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds (100..60000).


<a id="nestedatt--check--flow"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.flow`

Required:

- `start_url` (String) URL the browser opens before running the steps.
- `steps` (Attributes List) Ordered browser actions. Include at least one assert_* step so a broken flow fails. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--flow--steps))

Optional:

- `step_timeout_ms` (Number) Per-step wait for a selector in milliseconds (100..60000).
- `timeout_ms` (Number) Whole-flow timeout in milliseconds (1000..120000).
- `verify_tls` (Boolean)

<a id="nestedatt--check--flow--steps"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.flow.steps`

Required:

- `op` (String) Action: goto, click, fill, wait_for, assert_text, assert_url.

Optional:

- `contains` (String) Substring to assert (op = assert_text or assert_url).
- `selector` (String) CSS selector (op = click, fill, wait_for, or optionally assert_text).
- `url` (String) Navigation URL (op = goto).
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Text to fill (op = fill). Write-only: the API redacts it on read, so external changes are not detected. Reference an org secret as {{name}} for credentials.



<a id="nestedatt--check--http"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.http`

Required:

- `expected_status` (Attributes) Expected HTTP status matcher. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--http--expected_status))
- `url` (String) URL to request.

Optional:

- `basic_auth` (Attributes, Sensitive) HTTP basic auth. Write-only: the API never returns the value, so external changes to it are not detected. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--http--basic_auth))
- `bearer_token` (String, Sensitive) Bearer token. Write-only: the API never returns the value, so external changes to it are not detected.
- `body` (String) Request body.
- `expected_body_contains` (String) Substring the response body must contain.
- `follow_redirects` (Boolean)
- `headers` (Map of String) Request headers.
- `max_redirects` (Number)
- `method` (String) HTTP method (uppercase).
- `timeout_ms` (Number) Request timeout in milliseconds (100..60000).
- `verify_tls` (Boolean)

<a id="nestedatt--check--http--expected_status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.http.expected_status`

Required:

- `kind` (String) One of: exact, range, one_of.

Optional:

- `exact` (Number) Expected status when kind = exact.
- `one_of` (List of Number) Accepted statuses when kind = one_of.
- `range` (Attributes) Inclusive status range when kind = range. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--check--http--expected_status--range))

<a id="nestedatt--check--http--expected_status--range"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.http.expected_status.range`

Required:

- `max` (Number)
- `min` (Number)



<a id="nestedatt--check--http--basic_auth"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.http.basic_auth`

Required:

- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `username` (String, Sensitive)



<a id="nestedatt--check--tcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.tcp`

Required:

- `host` (String)
- `port` (Number) Port (1..65535).

Optional:

- `timeout_ms` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds (100..60000).


<a id="nestedatt--check--tls_cert"></a>
### Nested Schema for `check.tls_cert`

Required:

- `critical_days` (Number) Fail when the cert expires within this many days.
- `host` (String)
- `port` (Number) Port (1..65535).
- `warn_days` (Number) Warn when the cert expires within this many days.

Optional:

- `server_name` (String) SNI to send if different from host.
- `timeout_ms` (Number) Timeout in milliseconds (100..60000).



<a id="nestedatt--alerts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `alerts`

Required:

- `after_failures` (Number) Consecutive failed checks before alerting (1..1000000).
- `channel_id` (String) Notification channel id (UUID).

Optional:

- `notify_recovery` (Boolean) Send a recovery notification when the target comes back up.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing target by its UUID.
# Write-only secrets (check.basic_auth, check.bearer_token) cannot be read back
# from the API, so set them in config after import.
terraform import uptimepage_target.api 01h7m8z4n6v0e1m7v7y6x8x8x8
```
